NCT00866619.name: RTS,S Phase 3
NCT00866619.group: RTS,S/AS01 4-dose schedule (3-dose primary + 18-month booster) vs rabies-vaccine control in children 5-17 months at first dose; multi-country (Burkina Faso, Gabon, Ghana, Kenya, Malawi, Mozambique, Tanzania); cumulative incidence to month 48

allOutcomes.title: Clinical malaria episode by month 48 (per-protocol primary, IRR)

NCT03896724.name: R21 Phase 2b
NCT03896724.group: R21/Matrix-M (Matrix-M 50 ug high-dose adjuvant) 3-dose primary series vs rabies-vaccine control in children 5-17 months in Burkina Faso (Nanoro); 12-month follow-up

allOutcomes.title: Clinical malaria episode at 12 months (vaccine efficacy 77%, high-dose adjuvant arm)

NCT03896724.snippet: Source: Datoo MS et al. Lancet 2021;397:1809-1818 (R21/Matrix-M phase 2b Burkina Faso).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)

CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Healthy child aged 5-17 months at the time of first study vaccination~* Provide written Informed consent of parent/guardian~* Child and parent/guardian resident in the study area villages and anticipated to be available for vaccination and follow-up for 2 years following last dose of vaccination~Exclusion Criteria:~* Clinically significant skin disorder (psoriasis, contact dermatitis etc.), immunodeficiency, cardiovascular disease, respiratory disease, endocrine disorder, ...
